title: Samosa

seo.title: Authentic Pakistani Samosa Recipe - Easy & Crispy
seo.metaDescription: Master the art of making crispy, flavorful Pakistani Samosas at home with our easy-to-follow recipe. Perfect as an appetizer or snack for any occasion!
h2Headings: Pro Tips for the Best Results
seo.imageAlt: Plate of golden-brown, crispy authentic Pakistani Samosas with chutney
seo.imageCaption: Enjoy these perfectly fried, authentic Pakistani Samosas, a delightful appetizer for any gathering.

description: Crispy on the outside, savory and spiced on the inside, Pakistani Samosas are a beloved appetizer perfect for any gathering. These triangular pastry pockets, filled with a delicious potato and pea mixture, are a staple in Pakistani cuisine and a treat for the senses. Enjoy the authentic taste of Pakistan with this classic snack.

ingredients.notes: for the dough, plus more for frying

instructions.title: Prepare the Dough
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine flour, ajwain, and salt. Add ghee or oil and rub it into the flour until it resembles breadcrumbs. Gradually add water and knead into a firm, smooth dough. Cover and let it rest for at least 20 minutes.

instructions.title: Cook the Potato Filling
instructions.text: Heat 2 tablespoons of oil in a pan. Add cumin seeds and let them splutter. Add chopped onion and sauté until translucent. Stir in ginger-garlic paste and green chilies, cooking for another minute until fragrant. Add green peas and cook for 3-4 minutes.

instructions.title: Spice the Filling
instructions.text: Add coriander powder, red chili powder, turmeric powder, garam masala, amchur, and salt to the pan. Cook the spices for 1-2 minutes, stirring constantly, ensuring they dont burn. Add a splash of water if needed to prevent burning.

instructions.title: Combine and Cool the Filling
instructions.text: Add the mashed potatoes to the spiced mixture. Mix well, breaking any large potato chunks, until all ingredients are evenly combined. Stir in fresh coriander leaves. Remove from heat and let the filling cool completely. This is crucial for easy shaping.

instructions.title: Shape the Samosas
instructions.text: Divide the dough into equal small balls. Roll each ball into an oval shape, then cut it in half to form two semi-circles. Take one semi-circle, moisten the straight edge with water, and form a cone by overlapping the edges. Fill the cone with the potato mixture, then seal the top edges to create a triangular Samosa. Repeat for all dough and filling.

instructions.title: Fry the Samosas
instructions.text: Heat enough oil in a deep pan or wok for deep frying on medium-low heat. Once the oil is moderately hot (not too hot, or the Samosas will brown too quickly and not cook inside), carefully slide the Samosas into the oil. Fry them in batches, turning occasionally, until they are golden brown and crispy on all sides. This usually takes about 8-10 minutes per batch.

instructions.title: Serve Hot
instructions.text: Remove the fried Samosas with a slotted spoon and place them on paper towels to drain excess oil. Serve hot with mint chutney, tamarind chutney, or ketchup. Enjoy your authentic Pakistani Samosas!

tips: To achieve truly crispy Samosas, ensure your dough is firm and kneaded well. The oil for frying should be on medium-low heat initially, allowing the pastry to cook through before browning.
For a richer filling, you can add a pinch of black salt (kala namak) or pomegranate seeds (anardana) for an extra tangy flavor that is common in Pakistani street-style Samosas.

Samosas can be prepared in advance and frozen. To freeze, arrange uncooked Samosas on a tray, freeze until solid, then transfer to an airtight container or freezer bag. Fry from frozen, adding a few extra minutes to the cooking time.
article: Samosa is a beloved Pakistani Appetizers dish enjoyed across Pakistan. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
